subject: ap-chemistry
title: Chemical Equilibrium & Le Chatelier's Principle
stimulus:

Write the expression for the equilibrium constant, K , for the reaction.
a

parts.prompt:
In the box below, complete the Lewis electron-dot diagram for HCOOH. Show all bonding and nonbonding
valence electrons.
$H_2$ NNH 2 (aq )  H 2 O(l)  $H_2$ NNH 3  (aq )  $OH^-$ (aq ) $K_{b}$ = 1.3  10 − 6

In aqueous solution, the compound H NNH reacts according to the equation above. A 50.0 mL sample of
0.25 M H NNH (aq)is combined with a 50.0 mL sample of 0.25 M HCOOH(aq).
(i)Write the balanced net ionic equation for the reaction that occurs when H NNH is combined
with HCOOH.
(ii) Is the resulting solution acidic, basic, or neutral? Justify your answer.
Use a pencil or pen with black or dark blue ink only. Do NOT write your name. Do NOT write outside the box.

When a catalyst is added to a solution of HCOOH(aq ), the reaction represented by the following equation
occurs.
HCOOH (aq ) → H (g )  CO (g)
2 2

scoring_points.description: For the correct balanced equation (state symbols not required): 1 point

required_elements: For the correct balanced equation (state symbols not required): 1 point

scoring_points.description: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point
alternatives.correct_example: Acidic. The K of H NNH + is greater than the K of HCOO−, so the production of $H_2O$+(aq)

parts.prompt: Is the reaction a redox reaction? Justify your answer.

scoring_points.description: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point

required_elements: Provide a correct response from the accepted alternatives
alternatives.correct_example: Accept one of the following •Yes. The oxidation number of hydrogen changes from +1 in HCOOH to zero i n H2 •Yes. The oxidati on number of carbon changes from +2 in HCOOH to +4 in CO2

parts.prompt:
The reaction occurs inarigid 4.3 L vessel at 25°C, and the total pressure is monitored, as shown in the
graph above. The vessel originally did not containany gas. Calculate the number of moles of CO (g )
produced in the reaction. (Assume that the amount of CO (g )dissolved in the solution isnegligible.)

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value of the pressure of CO (may be implicit): 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value of the pressure of CO (may be implicit): 1 point
alternatives.correct_example: 24 atm total × 1 atm CO2 / 2 atm of product = 12 atm CO

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated number of moles of CO : 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated number of moles of CO : 1 point

parts.prompt:
After the reaction has proceeded for several minutes, does the amount of catalyst increase, decrease, or
remain the same? Justify your answer.
Use a pencil or pen with black or dark blue ink only. Do NOT write your name. Do NOT write outside the box.

scoring_points.description: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point
alternatives.correct_example: It would remain the same. In a catalyzed reaction the net amount of catalyst is constant.
